findings.title: Gate 3 trigger list falsely includes read-only analyze/phase
findings.stale_claim: Any keyword including analyze and phase triggers activation/spec-folder Gate 3.
findings.current_truth: The authoritative Gate 3 classifier intentionally omits analyze/decompose/phase from positive triggers; analyze is a read-only disqualifier.

findings.title: Rerank stage is not fully removed
findings.stale_claim: Memory search returns fused results with no rerank stage.
findings.current_truth: Only model-based cross-encoder/local-GGUF reranking was removed; Stage 3 still performs MMR diversity reranking and MPAB chunk collapse, and the memory_search rerank option defaults true.

findings.title: Dead Copilot hook README path
findings.stale_claim: Hook details live in mcp_server/hooks/copilot/README.md.
findings.current_truth: There is no hooks/copilot README in the source hook tree; the hooks README lists only claude and codex hook folders, with OpenCode handled by plugin bridge.

findings.title: Copilot prompt hook claim overstates current wiring
findings.stale_claim: Every prompt-submit hook in Claude, Codex, OpenCode, and Copilot calls into this package for its startup brief.
findings.current_truth: Current source hook folders are Claude/Codex; OpenCode uses the plugin bridge. The checked-in Copilot .github hook config points at a user-local Superset hook and is documented as test infrastructure only, with no executable code or subdirectories in that folder.

findings.title: Validation rule count is stale
findings.stale_claim: Run all 20 validation rules / validation reports across 20 rules.
findings.current_truth: The authoritative validator registry currently contains 37 rules.

findings.title: Background index scan handlers missing from handler map
findings.stale_claim: The handler tree maps memory_index_scan to memory-index.ts but omits status/cancel job handlers.
findings.current_truth: Background memory_index_scan is implemented with separate memory_index_scan_status and memory_index_scan_cancel handlers in memory-index-scan-jobs.ts.

findings.title: Search README still names cross-encoder/local reranking gates
findings.stale_claim: *rerank*.ts contains cross-encoder, local, and score reranking gates.
findings.current_truth: The model-based cross-encoder/local-GGUF reranking layer is removed; Stage 3 now performs algorithmic MMR diversity and MPAB chunk-collapse only.

findings.title: Shared package Node requirement is too low
findings.stale_claim: Node.js minimum is 18+.
findings.current_truth: The shared package engine requires Node >=20.11.0.

findings.title: Embedding auto-selection order is reversed
findings.stale_claim: Auto-cascade chooses Voyage when VOYAGE_API_KEY is set, then OpenAI, then ollama, then HF local.
findings.current_truth: Auto mode is local-first: explicit EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER is the only normal cloud auto-select path; otherwise persisted/reachable Ollama wins, then hf-local, with cloud only explicit or last-resort fallback.

findings.title: Old BGE/GGUF embedder defaults remain documented
findings.stale_claim: OLLAMA_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL defaults to unsloth/bge-base-en-v1.5-GGUF and HF_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL defaults to onnx-community/bge-base-en-v1.5-ONNX.
findings.current_truth: The local canonical fallback is nomic-embed-text-v1.5 for Ollama and nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5 for hf-local.

findings.title: Legacy slash command spelling
findings.stale_claim: Continuity recovery uses /spec_kit:resume.
findings.current_truth: The active command is /speckit:resume.

findings.title: Speckit command count is stale
findings.stale_claim: .opencode/commands/speckit/ contains Spec Kit command definitions (8 commands).
findings.current_truth: The current speckit command index lists seven command rows, and the speckit folder itself contains four speckit command markdown files plus README/assets.

findings.title: Prompt-submit hook surface is overstated
findings.stale_claim: Every prompt-submit hook in Claude, Codex, OpenCode, and Copilot calls into this package for its startup brief.
findings.current_truth: Claude and Codex prompt-submit shims now delegate advisor delivery to system-skill-advisor; startup context is handled separately by session-start/session-prime paths, and OpenCode uses the plugin bridge rather than a mcp_server prompt-submit hook.

findings.title: Default scan scope claim is wrong
findings.stale_claim: Default scope is end-user repo only and excludes .opencode/skills/** and active spec folders by default.
findings.current_truth: memory_index_scan defaults includeConstitutional=true for .opencode/skills/*/constitutional/ and includeSpecDocs=true for .opencode/specs/ documents; background scan status/cancel is also part of the current schema.

findings.title: Background scan job handlers missing
findings.stale_claim: The handler tree documents memory-index.ts as the scan handler but does not include the background memory_index_scan status/cancel handler module.
findings.current_truth: The background memory_index_scan status/cancel surface lives in handlers/memory-index-scan-jobs.ts and backs memory_index_scan_status plus memory_index_scan_cancel.

findings.title: memory_health count-field claim is stale
findings.stale_claim: memory_health data.routing does not currently expose per-channel count fields.
findings.current_truth: memory_health data.routing now includes channelInvocationCounts and channelInvocationRates, plus graph and degree contribution counters.

findings.title: Lifecycle tool routing understated
findings.stale_claim: lifecycle-tools.ts routes async ingest start, status and cancel calls.
findings.current_truth: lifecycle-tools.ts routes memory_index_scan, memory_index_scan_status, memory_index_scan_cancel, task learning tools, async ingest tools, eval tools, and session tools.

findings.title: Hook tree omits warm CLI fallback helpers
findings.stale_claim: The hooks directory tree lists runtime folders and helper modules but omits the warm daemon CLI fallback helpers.
findings.current_truth: hooks/ contains spec-memory-cli-fallback.ts, code-index-cli-fallback.ts, and warm-cli-fallback-envelope.ts for bounded warm-only CLI recovery.

findings.title: Resume slash command name is stale
findings.stale_claim: The wrapper docs say /spec_kit:resume restores packet context.
findings.current_truth: The current command surface is /speckit:resume, registered by system-spec-kit and backed by .opencode/commands/speckit/resume.md.

findings.title: Spec-memory CLI tool count is stale
findings.stale_claim: spec-memory.cjs exposes 37 tools; later lines also expect spec-memory=37.
findings.current_truth: spec-memory exposes 39 tools; list-tools parity tests assert 39 and the registry includes memory_index_scan_status and memory_index_scan_cancel.

findings.title: Memory tool reference count is stale
findings.stale_claim: Tool Reference says 37 mk-spec-memory tools and public surface is 37 descriptors.
findings.current_truth: TOOL_DEFINITIONS is currently 39 tools.

findings.title: Strict schema flag describes old tool surface
findings.stale_claim: SPECKIT_STRICT_SCHEMAS enforces validation for the 37-tool mk-spec-memory surface.
findings.current_truth: The mk-spec-memory surface is 39 tools.

findings.title: Indexed source count omits graph metadata
findings.stale_claim: Current baseline says 2 active indexed content sources plus retired compatibility artifacts.
findings.current_truth: memory_index_scan discovers constitutional files, spec documents, and graph-metadata.json files, merges all three, and graph-metadata.json has its own graph_metadata document type.

findings.title: memory_index_scan parameter docs miss background jobs
findings.stale_claim: memory_index_scan parameters list only specFolder, force, includeConstitutional, includeSpecDocs, and incremental.
findings.current_truth: memory_index_scan also supports background:true and governance/retention fields, and background jobs are polled/cancelled through memory_index_scan_status and memory_index_scan_cancel.

findings.title: Save workflow cites old schema version
findings.stale_claim: Spec documents use schema v23 fields.
findings.current_truth: The current vector index schema version is v37.

findings.title: Legacy memory folder documented as active
findings.stale_claim: Level sections and the memory/ special folder describe memory/ as context preservation for future sessions with DD-MM-YY files.
findings.current_truth: The current save workflow skips legacy [spec]/memory/*.md writes; canonical continuity lives in packet docs and graph/description metadata.

findings.title: SpecKit command namespace is stale
findings.stale_claim: Command map uses /spec_kit:resume, /spec_kit:plan, /spec_kit:implement, and /spec_kit:complete.
findings.current_truth: Current command files are under .opencode/commands/speckit/ and current gates reference /speckit:resume.

findings.title: Hook fallback names old resume command
findings.stale_claim: Hook fallback tells operators to use /spec_kit:resume.
findings.current_truth: The current command namespace is /speckit, with resume implemented at .opencode/commands/speckit/resume.md.

findings.title: Deep command map omits current modes
findings.stale_claim: SpecKit workflow command map lists only /deep:research and /deep:review.
findings.current_truth: The deep-loop registry includes context, research, review, ai-council, and agent-improvement modes with commands /deep:context, /deep:research, /deep:review, /deep:ai-council, and /deep:agent-improvement.

findings.title: Agent roster omits current deep-loop agents
findings.stale_claim: Dispatch header agent enum lists @context, @code, @review, @debug, @markdown, @ai-council, and @deep-research.
findings.current_truth: Current OpenCode agents also include deep-context, deep-review, deep-improvement, prompt-improver, and orchestrate.

findings.title: Continuity edit rule conflicts with current gate
findings.stale_claim: Do not use Write/Edit tools to author continuity surfaces directly.
findings.current_truth: AGENTS.md explicitly allows quick direct edits to _memory.continuity in implementation-summary.md without generate-context.js.

findings.title: generate-context flow overstates indexing
findings.stale_claim: The canonical save flow ends with "context artifacts and index are updated" and the outputs table says memory index updates and embedding rows are written through the CLI flow.
findings.current_truth: Step 11.5 does not always index. When the mk-spec-memory daemon is alive, standalone indexing is skipped to avoid a second SQLite writer and the caller is told to finish via MCP memory_index_scan.

findings.title: root topology omits active script zones
findings.stale_claim: The root package topology lists the script folder structure but omits current README-bearing zones such as config/ and validation/.
findings.current_truth: config/ is the current config barrel boundary and validation/ contains active validators used by validation flow, pre-commit and write-time comment hygiene.

findings.title: spec script tree omits current entrypoints
findings.stale_claim: The spec/ package topology lists lifecycle shell scripts but omits scaffold-debug-delegation.sh, check-smart-router.sh and the legacy test-validation.sh wrapper.
findings.current_truth: Those files exist and are current: debug-delegation scaffold generation, SKILL.md smart-router validation and a wrapper forwarding to scripts/tests/test-validation.sh.

findings.title: setup topology omits installer helper surface
findings.stale_claim: The setup/ package topology only lists prerequisite, native-module and Node-version helper scripts.
findings.current_truth: setup/ also ships install.sh plus _utils.sh. install.sh is a Spec Kit Memory MCP installer that installs dependencies, builds the TypeScript workspace and runs native-module health checks.

findings.title: Constitutional rule count and topology are stale
findings.stale_claim: README says the folder contains 13 active rule files and lists an older rule set.
findings.current_truth: The current folder has 18 non-README constitutional rule files, including newer rules not listed such as fable-governor, automated-writers-never-overwrite-manual, regression-baseline-and-delta, finding-is-a-hypothesis, recursion-control, and entity-cooccurrence-is-not-causal.

findings.title: Example directory names are wrong
findings.stale_claim: Directory tree lists examples as level-1, level-2, level-3, level-3-plus, and phase-parent.
findings.current_truth: Rendered examples currently use level_1, level_2, level_3, and level_3+; phase-parent is a manifest template contract, not an examples/phase-parent directory.

findings.title: Examples README points to non-existent copy-source folders
findings.stale_claim: README says to copy from sibling level_* template folders and links ../level_1/README.md, ../level_2/README.md, ../level_3/README.md, and ../level_3+/README.md.
findings.current_truth: Current source templates live under templates/manifest/*.md.tmpl and the manifest maps public levels to those templates; there are no sibling template folders with README files at those paths.

findings.title: Changelog README uses obsolete resume command spelling
findings.stale_claim: Recovery still flows through `/spec_kit:resume`.
findings.current_truth: The current command surface is `/speckit:resume`, implemented by .opencode/commands/speckit/resume.md.

findings.title: Shared README uses obsolete resume command spelling
findings.stale_claim: `/spec_kit:resume` rebuilds active context from handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs.
findings.current_truth: The current command surface is `/speckit:resume`, implemented by .opencode/commands/speckit/resume.md.

findings.title: Shared package inventory omits current modules
findings.stale_claim: README says there are 8 top-level TS modules and lists an older shared/ structure without newer daemon/contract modules.
findings.current_truth: The shared package now exports and contains additional top-level modules such as budget-allocator, compact-merger, and code-graph-contracts, plus ipc/ and predicates/ subdirectories.

findings.title: Node minimum is stale
findings.stale_claim: Requirements table says Node.js minimum is 18+ and 20+ is recommended.
findings.current_truth: The current shared package requires Node >=20.11.0.

findings.title: Embedding defaults are stale
findings.stale_claim: README lists OLLAMA_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL default as unsloth/bge-base-en-v1.5-GGUF, OLLAMA_EMBEDDINGS_GGUF_FILE as bge-base-en-v1.5-300M-Q8_0.gguf, HF_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL as onnx-community/bge-base-en-v1.5-ONNX, and MEMORY_AUTO_MIGRATE_HF_TO_LLAMA as an enabled migration flag.
findings.current_truth: Current defaults derive from the registry: ollama uses nomic-embed-text-v1.5 / nomic-embed-text:v1.5, hf-local uses nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5, and the old HF-to-llama migration flag is not present in the current TS code path.

findings.title: Provider precedence contradicts current local-first cascade
findings.stale_claim: Provider selection precedence says auto-cascade tries Voyage first, then OpenAI, then Ollama, then HF local.
findings.current_truth: Current auto-select is local-first: ollama, then hf-local, then openai, then voyage; explicit EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER is tried first and then falls back to that cascade.

findings.title: memory_index_scan lease behavior is stale
findings.stale_claim: The catalog says lease_active/cooldown guards return an E429 response with a wait time.
findings.current_truth: Current memory_index_scan coalesces active/cooldown scans into a success envelope with coalesced:true/status:'coalesced'; the public schema also exposes background:true plus memory_index_scan_status and memory_index_scan_cancel.

findings.title: Embedding provider table is cloud-first/BGE stale
findings.stale_claim: The catalog says local fallback is BGE and auto provider precedence is VOYAGE_API_KEY -> OPENAI_API_KEY -> local ollama -> hf-local.
findings.current_truth: Provider resolution is local-first: explicit EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER is the only direct cloud selector, then persisted/reachable Ollama, then hf-local; the local default is nomic-embed-text-v1.5 and hf-local defaults to nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5.

findings.title: Embedding/API scenario expects obsolete cloud-key precedence
findings.stale_claim: The playbook scenario asks operators to validate provider rules including cloud key precedence.
findings.current_truth: Cloud providers are not selected just because API keys exist; local-first auto mode prefers Ollama/hf-local unless EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER explicitly selects a cloud provider or a later fallback path is reached.

findings.title: Local-LLM README hard-codes retired BGE defaults
findings.stale_claim: The README describes BGE local fallback via Ollama and a BGE GGUF cache path as the embedding backbone.
findings.current_truth: The current local default is Ollama nomic-embed-text-v1.5; hf-local defaults to nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5 and downloads through the Hugging Face hub cache.

findings.title: MCP usage model ignores daemon-backed CLI front door
findings.stale_claim: The README says Memory MCP is never used by humans typing directly and is always used by AI assistants invoking each other.
findings.current_truth: The memory surface is dual-stack: spec-memory.cjs is a full-parity CLI front door for hooks, cron, CI, operator shell diagnostics, and transport-down recovery in addition to MCP sessions.

findings.title: README allows invalid per-scenario PARTIAL verdict
findings.stale_claim: The category README lists PARTIAL as a scenario grading outcome.
findings.current_truth: The root playbook allows PASS, FAIL, SKIP, and UNAUTOMATABLE for scenarios; PARTIAL is only a packet-level or aggregate feature state.

findings.title: Doctor README omits current route targets
findings.stale_claim: The README frames the /doctor command surface as the listed memory, causal-graph, deep-loop, code-graph, update, migration, and mcp scenarios.
findings.current_truth: The current route manifest also includes embeddings, skill-advisor, skill-budget, parent-skill, and fable-mode targets.

findings.title: Daemon re-election default source is misstated
findings.stale_claim: The catalog says daemon re-election is default-on in committed runtime configs.
findings.current_truth: Re-election is still default-on, but the launcher code is now the source of that default and runtime configs no longer set SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION.

findings.title: advisor_recommend option list is incomplete
findings.stale_claim: `advisor_recommend` accepts three options: `topK`, `includeAttribution`, and `includeAbstainReasons`.
findings.current_truth: `advisor_recommend.options` currently also accepts `confidenceThreshold` and `uncertaintyThreshold`, so the live schema has five option fields.

findings.title: Trusted mutation list omits propagate apply
findings.stale_claim: Explicit trusted mutation paths remain `advisor_rebuild` and `skill_graph_scan`.
findings.current_truth: The CLI also requires trusted authority for real `skill_graph_propagate_enhances` apply writes (`mode: apply` with `dryRun !== true`).

findings.title: Propagate tool is not wholly internal or trust-gated
findings.stale_claim: The server exposes nine tools, eight public plus one trusted-caller-gated internal tool.
findings.current_truth: All 9 tool definitions are returned by ListTools; `skill_graph_propagate_enhances` is only trust-gated for the mutating apply path, while report/propose and dry-run apply are read-safe.

findings.title: advisor_validate thresholds are stale
findings.stale_claim: Troubleshooting says corpus top-1 below 80.5% is the regression line, and verification says holdout top-1 is 77.5%.
findings.current_truth: The current validation thresholds are `FULL_CORPUS_THRESHOLD = 0.75` and `HOLDOUT_THRESHOLD = 0.725`.

findings.title: SKILL.md misclassifies the ninth tool as internal
findings.stale_claim: `mk_skill_advisor` registers 8 public tools plus 1 internal trusted-caller tool.
findings.current_truth: The MCP server lists all 9 tools; `skill_graph_propagate_enhances` is only trust-gated when it performs a real apply write.

findings.title: Tools README overstates propagate trust gate
findings.stale_claim: `skill_graph_propagate_enhances` proposes missing inbound edges under a trusted-caller gate.
findings.current_truth: The handler only requires trusted caller authority for `mode === 'apply' && dryRun !== true`; report, propose, and dry-run apply remain open read-safe paths.

findings.title: Handlers README uses removed skill_advisor path
findings.stale_claim: `skill_advisor/handlers/` contains MCP tool handlers.
findings.current_truth: Handlers live directly under `mcp_server/handlers/`; the tool registry imports `../handlers/index.js`.

findings.title: Library README uses removed skill_advisor path
findings.stale_claim: `skill_advisor/lib/` owns the TypeScript helper layer.
findings.current_truth: Runtime helpers live directly under `mcp_server/lib/`; handlers import `../lib/...` modules.

findings.title: Schemas README uses removed skill_advisor path
findings.stale_claim: `skill_advisor/schemas/` defines contract shapes.
findings.current_truth: Schemas live directly under `mcp_server/schemas/`; handlers import `../schemas/advisor-tool-schemas.js`.

findings.title: Library topology count is stale
findings.stale_claim: `lib/` carries runtime helpers across 11 subdirectories and lists only scorer, daemon, freshness, lifecycle, derived, compat, auth, corpus, cross-skill-edges, context, and shadow.
findings.current_truth: Current runtime code also imports package-local `lib/skill-graph/`, `lib/embedders/`, and `lib/ipc/`; the live lib directory also contains additional subdirectories such as `shared/` and `utils/`.

findings.title: Architecture diagram uses wrong database filename
findings.stale_claim: The diagram labels the database as `database/skill-graph.db`.
findings.current_truth: The current database filename is `skill-graph.sqlite`.

findings.title: Scorer directory tree omits feedback calibration module
findings.stale_claim: The scorer directory tree lists the scorer modules but omits `feedback-calibration.ts`.
findings.current_truth: `feedback-calibration.ts` exists as the Advisor Feedback Calibration Reducer module in `lib/scorer/`.

findings.title: Validation command uses unsupported Vitest flag
findings.stale_claim: Run `npm test -- --runInBand`.
findings.current_truth: The package test script is Vitest; current serial execution uses `--no-file-parallelism --maxWorkers=1`, and `--runInBand` is rejected as `CACError: Unknown option --runInBand`.
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/package.json:11; .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/README.md:301-305; command output from `npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server exec -- vitest run --runInBand ...`

findings.title: Architecture diagram names dead SQLite file
findings.stale_claim: The database node is labeled `skill-graph.db`.
findings.current_truth: The runtime database filename is `skill-graph.sqlite`.

findings.title: Lane README omits BM25 shadow lane
findings.stale_claim: The folder has 5 code files and lists only `derived.ts`, `explicit.ts`, `graph-causal.ts`, `lexical.ts` and `semantic-shadow.ts`.
findings.current_truth: The folder now also contains an active `bm25.ts` BM25F lexical shadow lane used by `lexical.ts` and registered as a shadow lane.

findings.title: Library subdirectory count is stale
findings.stale_claim: `lib/` carries runtime helpers across 11 subdirectories and lists an older subset.
findings.current_truth: The library now has additional active subpackages including IPC, skill-graph, embedders, shared and utils paths; the 11-subdirectory summary is no longer current.

findings.title: Handler README uses legacy pseudo-path
findings.stale_claim: `skill_advisor/handlers/` contains MCP tool handlers.
findings.current_truth: The current TypeScript package path is under `mcp_server/handlers/`.

findings.title: Schema README uses legacy pseudo-path
findings.stale_claim: `skill_advisor/schemas/` defines contract shapes.
findings.current_truth: The current schema sources are under `mcp_server/schemas/`.

findings.title: Library README uses legacy pseudo-path
findings.stale_claim: `skill_advisor/lib/` owns the TypeScript helper layer.
findings.current_truth: The current library sources are under `mcp_server/lib/`.

findings.title: Scorer tree omits feedback calibration module
findings.stale_claim: The scorer directory tree lists scorer files but omits `feedback-calibration.ts`.
findings.current_truth: `feedback-calibration.ts` is a current scorer module for the advisor feedback calibration reducer.

findings.title: Scripts README uses legacy pseudo-path
findings.stale_claim: `skill_advisor/scripts/` holds command-line utilities.
findings.current_truth: The current scripts are under `mcp_server/scripts/`.

findings.title: Scripts tree omits current maintenance scripts
findings.stale_claim: The directory tree lists older scripts and omits current doc-frontmatter and zombie-soak scripts.
findings.current_truth: `check-skill-doc-frontmatter.mjs`, `check-skill-doc-frontmatter.sh` and `verify-zombie-soak.sh` are current scripts in this package.

findings.title: Tests README uses legacy pseudo-path
findings.stale_claim: `skill_advisor/tests/` is the regression surface.
findings.current_truth: The current tests live under `mcp_server/tests/`.

findings.title: Tests directory tree is materially incomplete
findings.stale_claim: The directory tree lists only a small older set of package-level test files.
findings.current_truth: Current package-level tests include newer daemon, launcher, CLI, parity, graph and harvest suites not represented in the tree, including the env-gated tri-daemon drill.

findings.title: Blast-radius response fields are wrong
findings.stale_claim: Quick start says `blast_radius` returns `affectedSymbols`, `affectedFiles` and readiness metadata.
findings.current_truth: `code_graph_query` blast_radius builds a payload with `sourceFiles`, `nodes`, `affectedFiles`, `depthGroups`, `riskLevel`, `minConfidence` and ambiguity fields; `affectedSymbols` is a `detect_changes` field, not a blast-radius output.

findings.title: Markdown is not in the default scan
findings.stale_claim: README says default scan globs include markdown, JSON, YAML and TOML as the doc lane.
findings.current_truth: Default include globs deliberately omit `**/*.md`; JSON/JSONC/YAML/YML/TOML remain default doc rows, while Markdown/prose docs require explicit `includeGlobs`.

findings.title: Old plugin broken-import troubleshooting remains
findings.stale_claim: Troubleshooting says plugin bridge startup can fail with missing `dist/handlers/session-resume.js` and points to a broken-import table.
findings.current_truth: The bridge no longer imports session-resume internals; it routes through `.opencode/bin/code-index.cjs` and the launcher IPC bridge.

findings.title: Doc-lane coverage overstates Markdown indexing
findings.stale_claim: Glossary says Markdown, JSON, YAML and TOML files are included as inventory rows only.
findings.current_truth: Default scan excludes Markdown/prose docs; config formats are included as `language='doc'` rows by default, and Markdown is only re-addable through explicit scan include globs.

findings.title: Lib description still mentions vector index
findings.stale_claim: Directory tree describes `lib/` as `Search, vector index, session and utility code`.
findings.current_truth: The library barrel exports code-graph modules such as indexer, DB, lease, seed/context, parser, readiness and startup helpers; there is no vector-index export in this package.

findings.title: Plugin bridge dependency direction points at dist
findings.stale_claim: Allowed dependency direction says `plugin_bridges/ -> dist/ (compiled runtime)`.
findings.current_truth: The plugin bridge routes through the committed CLI shim and launcher IPC bridge rather than importing code-index `dist/` runtime modules directly.

findings.title: DATABASE_DIR consumer list omits server entrypoint
findings.stale_claim: Current consumers are listed as `lib/code-graph-db.ts`, `lib/apply-orchestrator.ts` and `lib/recovery-procedures.ts`.
findings.current_truth: `mcp_server/index.ts` also imports `DATABASE_DIR` and uses it for owner-lease refresh and IPC socket path resolution.

findings.title: Config doc rows are not .opencode-only
findings.stale_claim: README says JSON/JSONC/YAML/YML/TOML config files can be registered as doc rows when `.opencode/` folders are explicitly opted in, and Markdown docs are not indexed.
findings.current_truth: Default include globs include JSON/JSONC/YAML/YML/TOML generally; Markdown is excluded from defaults but `detectLanguage()` still maps `.md` to `doc` if a caller explicitly includes it.

findings.title: Library tree omits active modules
findings.stale_claim: Directory tree presents current `lib/` topology but omits active modules such as `symbol-bm25-resolver.ts`, `cross-file-edge-resolver.ts` and `config-defaults.ts`.
findings.current_truth: Those modules exist and own BM25 symbol fallback, cross-file call edge resolution and config defaults.

findings.title: Secondary-client default is 64, not 8
findings.stale_claim: README says secondary clients are capped by `SPECKIT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS`, defaulting to `8`.
findings.current_truth: `DEFAULT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS` is `64`.

findings.title: Plugin bridge no longer calls session resume
findings.stale_claim: README says the bridge initializes code-graph runtime, calls session resume and outputs a transport payload.
findings.current_truth: The bridge defaults to `code-graph-status`, blocks maintenance tools, warm-probes the daemon and spawns `code-index.cjs --warm-only`; session-resume remains outside this bridge.

findings.title: Naming note points to old packet ADR
findings.stale_claim: Naming note says to see ADR-002 in the 036 packet.
findings.current_truth: Current name mapping is documented in `references/runtime/naming_conventions.md`, including plugin bridge, CLI shim and `mk-code-index` identifiers.

findings.title: Generated code README points to 026 audit output
findings.stale_claim: Audit example says expected output is findings recorded in the 026 audit report; troubleshooting says rerun the 026 audit check.
findings.current_truth: The folder now contains current `code_graph_classify_query_intent` dispatch tests for the 8-tool surface; validation should use package tests, not a historical 026 audit.

findings.title: Convergence output shape is stale
findings.stale_claim: The quick start says convergence output is a JSON object with decision, signals, blockers and stopBlocked.
findings.current_truth: convergence.cjs emits a wrapper with status/data plus workflow-facing fields graph_decision, graph_signals_json, graph_blockers_json, graph_stop_blocked, graph_trace_json and graph_convergence_score.

findings.title: Scorer and signal names are wrong
findings.stale_claim: The README says bayesian-scorer interprets novelty signals into typed convergence decisions, and coverage-graph-signals extracts novelty rate, claim-support ratio and contradiction count.
findings.current_truth: bayesian-scorer only exports computeScore and shouldDemote; convergence.cjs builds decisions, and coverage-graph-signals exposes typed research/review/context metrics including questionCoverage, claimVerificationRate, contradictionDensity, sliceCoverage, reuseCatalogCoverage and agreementRate.

findings.title: Loop type troubleshooting omits current modes
findings.stale_claim: The troubleshooting row says input validation should check --loop-type is review or research.
findings.current_truth: The runtime scripts validate exactly research, review, council or context; improvement is host-driven and not a runtime loopType.

findings.title: Library consumer roster is pre-hub
findings.stale_claim: The library README says shared domain logic is for three deep-* sibling skills: deep-review, deep-research and deep-ai-council, and says deep-loop applies to all three deep-* skills.
findings.current_truth: Current routing is through deep-loop-workflows with context, research, review, ai-council and four improvement lanes; runtimeLoopType is research|review|council|context for graph-backed modes and null for improvement lanes.

findings.title: Coverage graph omits context mode
findings.stale_claim: The coverage-graph README describes convergence primitives only for deep-review and deep-research and lists only review/research consumers.
findings.current_truth: coverage-graph-db defines LoopType as research|review|context and includes context node kinds/relations; coverage-graph-signals computes context signals: sliceCoverage, reuseCatalogCoverage, agreementRate, relevanceFloor and dependencyCompleteness.

findings.title: Command surface still says speckit deep
findings.stale_claim: The scripts README says runtime scripts are consumed by /speckit:deep-* workflows.
findings.current_truth: The current command surface is /deep:* under .opencode/commands/deep, and deep-loop-workflows documents /deep:context, /deep:research, /deep:review, /deep:ai-council and four /deep:* improvement commands.

findings.title: Script inventory is incomplete
findings.stale_claim: The scripts table lists only convergence.cjs, query.cjs, status.cjs and upsert.cjs, and convergence is described as review/research only.
findings.current_truth: The runtime scripts directory also has fanout-run, fanout-pool, fanout-salvage, fanout-merge and loop-lock entry points, and graph scripts accept research, review, council and context loop types.

findings.title: Database README omits council graph store
findings.stale_claim: The database README says persistence is read/written via lib/coverage-graph and lists only deep-loop-graph.sqlite.
findings.current_truth: The database folder also contains council-graph.sqlite, owned by lib/council/council-graph-db.ts, and scripts switch to council graph modules for loopType=council.

findings.title: Writer-lock implementation is misattributed
findings.stale_claim: The database lifecycle says it is backed by atomic-write and flock locking via lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.
findings.current_truth: Graph writer locking is implemented by scripts/lib/cli-guards.cjs acquireWriterLock using wx lock-file acquisition, stale-lock reclamation and nonce ownership; atomic-state is not the DB writer-lock implementation.

findings.title: Helper surface table misses evidence contract
findings.stale_claim: The helper surface table lists loop-lock, jsonl-repair, atomic-state, executor config/audit, validation, prompt-pack, permissions, scorer, fallback router, runtime-capabilities, artifact-root and lifecycle-taxonomy.
findings.current_truth: lib/deep-loop also contains evidence-contract.ts, a machine-checkable dispatch-boundary evidence metadata validator.

findings.title: Top-level test map omits council graph integration
findings.stale_claim: The top-level tests README describes integration tests as direct script invocation and review-depth coverage-graph fixtures.
findings.current_truth: The integration suite also includes council graph script coverage and council graph value scenarios.

findings.title: Unit test README is materially incomplete
findings.stale_claim: The unit README says unit tests cover lib/deep-loop and lib/coverage-graph only, and its table stops at the older core module list.
findings.current_truth: The current unit suite also covers lifecycle taxonomy, host-driven improvement, CLI writer locks, fanout, evidence-contract, council-graph-query and related runtime contracts.

findings.title: Integration test table misses council graph tests
findings.stale_claim: The integration README contents table lists only convergence/upsert/query/status scripts plus review-depth graph, convergence and validator fixtures.
findings.current_truth: The current integration suite includes council-graph-script.vitest.ts and council-graph-value-scenarios.vitest.ts for council graph CLI behavior and value scenarios.

findings.title: Advisor runtime does not read mode registry
findings.stale_claim: `mode-registry.json` is the single source of truth; the hub, commands, and advisor all read it and none re-derive the mapping.
findings.current_truth: The registry is the source projection, but the advisor keeps hardcoded Python/TypeScript projection maps in sync via a CI drift guard and explicitly does not read `mode-registry.json` at runtime.

findings.title: Council persistence ownership is stale
findings.stale_claim: The caller writes artifacts from the council report by running `persist-artifacts.cjs`.
findings.current_truth: The `@ai-council` agent is scoped-write and persists the canonical `ai-council/**` artifact set directly; the CLI helper is fallback for non-council callers, and a dispatching parent does not need to invoke it for normal persistence.

findings.title: Deep-loop roster omits improvement mode
findings.stale_claim: The council shares `deep-loop-runtime` with three sibling skills / all four deep loops.
findings.current_truth: The current deep-loop workflow roster has five top-level personas: context, research, review, ai-council, and improvement; improvement includes four command lanes.

findings.title: Deep-loop roster omits improvement mode
findings.stale_claim: `deep-context` shares `deep-loop-runtime` with three sibling skills.
findings.current_truth: The current deep-loop workflow roster has five top-level personas: context, research, review, ai-council, and improvement; improvement includes four command lanes.

findings.title: Deep-loop roster omits improvement mode
findings.stale_claim: All four sibling loops share `deep-loop-runtime`, and the sibling list names only context/review/ai-council.
findings.current_truth: The current deep-loop workflow roster has five top-level personas: context, research, review, ai-council, and improvement; improvement includes four command lanes.

findings.title: Deep-loop roster omits improvement mode
findings.stale_claim: All four sibling loops share `deep-loop-runtime`, and the sibling list names only research/context/ai-council.
findings.current_truth: The current deep-loop workflow roster has five top-level personas: context, research, review, ai-council, and improvement; improvement includes four command lanes.

findings.title: ai-council scripts README omits active orchestrators
findings.stale_claim: The README says this folder has 3 code files and lists only advise-council-completion.cjs, persist-artifacts.cjs, and replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs.
findings.current_truth: The folder also contains orchestrate-session.cjs and orchestrate-topic.cjs, so the direct script set is 5 code files.

findings.title: ai-council lib README omits findings registry
findings.stale_claim: The README says scripts/lib has 3 code files and lists only audit-trail.cjs, persist-artifacts.cjs, and rollback.cjs.
findings.current_truth: scripts/lib also contains findings-registry.cjs, so the direct library set is 4 code files.

findings.title: deep-context scripts README omits loop-lock
findings.stale_claim: The README says there is 1 code file, reduce-state.cjs.
findings.current_truth: deep-context/scripts also contains loop-lock.cjs, a host-facing wrapper for runtime loop locking.

findings.title: deep-improvement scripts still describe two lanes
findings.stale_claim: The README describes scripts for the two deep-improvement lanes, Lane A agent-improvement and Lane B model-benchmark.
findings.current_truth: The current loop host supports agent-improvement, model-benchmark, skill-benchmark, and non-dev-ai-system-refine.

findings.title: shared README says it serves both lanes only
findings.stale_claim: The README says shared/ is used by BOTH lanes: Lane A agent-improvement and Lane B model-benchmark.
findings.current_truth: shared/loop-host.cjs now routes four modes, including skill-benchmark and non-dev-ai-system-refine.

findings.title: shared directory tree omits active shared scripts
findings.stale_claim: The README tree lists only loop-host, promote-candidate, materialize-benchmark-fixtures, reduce-state, improvement-journal, mutation-coverage, and README.md.
findings.current_truth: shared/ also contains active helpers such as fixture-lint.cjs, extract-deliverable.cjs, parse-args.cjs, model-family.cjs, and rubric-guard.cjs.

findings.title: mirror sync runtime count contradicts code
findings.stale_claim: The frontmatter describes mirror-sync-verify.cjs as a 4-runtime mirror sync verifier.
findings.current_truth: mirror-sync-verify.cjs defines three runtime mirrors: opencode, claude, and codex.

findings.title: agent integration README claims four mirrors
findings.stale_claim: The README says scan-integration.cjs scans the four runtime mirrors.
findings.current_truth: scan-integration.cjs currently defines three mirror templates: .opencode, .claude, and .codex.

findings.title: model-benchmark tree omits sweep support
findings.stale_claim: The README tree lists only dispatch-model.cjs, run-benchmark.cjs, and scorer/.
findings.current_truth: model-benchmark also contains sweep-benchmark.cjs and a lib/ subtree for sweep/profile helpers.

findings.title: cross-skill related links resolve to dead paths
findings.stale_claim: The README links sk-code and sk-doc via ../../sk-code and ../../sk-doc from inside deep-loop-workflows/ai-council/scripts.
findings.current_truth: The actual skill roots are .opencode/skills/sk-code and .opencode/skills/sk-doc; the README's relative paths resolve under deep-loop-workflows/sk-code and deep-loop-workflows/sk-doc, which do not exist.

findings.title: nested lib cross-skill links resolve to dead paths
findings.stale_claim: The README links sk-code and sk-doc via ../../../sk-code and ../../../sk-doc from scripts/lib.
findings.current_truth: Those paths still resolve under deep-loop-workflows/, while the actual skill roots are siblings of deep-loop-workflows under .opencode/skills/.

findings.title: deep-context scripts cross-skill links are dead
findings.stale_claim: The README links sk-code and sk-doc via ../../sk-code and ../../sk-doc.
findings.current_truth: From deep-context/scripts those links resolve under deep-loop-workflows/, not to the actual .opencode/skills/sk-code and .opencode/skills/sk-doc roots.

findings.title: deep-research scripts cross-skill links are dead
findings.stale_claim: The README links sk-code and sk-doc via ../../sk-code and ../../sk-doc.
findings.current_truth: From deep-research/scripts those links resolve under deep-loop-workflows/, not to the actual .opencode/skills/sk-code and .opencode/skills/sk-doc roots.

findings.title: deep-review scripts cross-skill links are dead
findings.stale_claim: The README links sk-code and sk-doc via ../../sk-code and ../../sk-doc.
findings.current_truth: From deep-review/scripts those links resolve under deep-loop-workflows/, not to the actual .opencode/skills/sk-code and .opencode/skills/sk-doc roots.

findings.title: deep-improvement scripts sk-code link is dead
findings.stale_claim: The README links sk-code/SKILL.md via ../../sk-code/SKILL.md.
findings.current_truth: From deep-improvement/scripts that resolves under deep-loop-workflows/, while the actual sk-code skill is .opencode/skills/sk-code/SKILL.md.

findings.title: Reproduce command writes output to wrong relative path
findings.stale_claim: The reproduce block says to cd into .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts and then pass --outputs-dir .opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/d4r-live.
findings.current_truth: run-skill-benchmark resolves --outputs-dir with path.resolve() relative to the current working directory, so after that cd the relative .opencode/... path resolves under the scripts directory, not the repo root.

findings.title: Benchmark corpus count omits current design-restraint scenarios
findings.stale_claim: Corpus is sk-code manual_testing_playbook with 24 scenarios: 15 routing, 2 advisor, 7 browser.
findings.current_truth: The current sk-code manual testing playbook has 28 deterministic scenarios across 8 categories, including DR-001..DR-004.

findings.title: Latest router aggregate is stale
findings.stale_claim: Latest router verdict is FAIL, aggregate 40.
findings.current_truth: The current router-final benchmark report says FAIL, aggregate 44/100.

findings.title: Related command path is dead
findings.stale_claim: Related document points to ../../../commands/deep/start-skill-benchmark-loop.
findings.current_truth: The current command surface is /deep:skill-benchmark in .opencode/commands/deep/skill-benchmark.md.

findings.title: Code-file count misses stack-folder validator
findings.stale_claim: Code files: 2, listing only test_verify_alignment_drift.py and verify_alignment_drift.py.
findings.current_truth: The folder also contains verify_stack_folders.py.

findings.title: Template file inventory omits CSS and HTML templates
findings.stale_claim: Code files: 1 and structure only lists component_template.js.
findings.current_truth: The direct template files also include component_template.css, form_scaffold_template.html, embed_template.html and head_footer_code_template.html.

findings.title: Obsolete 026 audit handoff
findings.stale_claim: Expected output is findings recorded in the 026 audit report, and troubleshooting says to rerun the 026 audit check.
findings.current_truth: 027 is the current refinement/release-alignment context; 026 is described as the completed predecessor.

findings.title: Obsolete 026 audit handoff
findings.stale_claim: Expected output is findings recorded in the 026 audit report, and troubleshooting says to rerun the 026 audit check.
findings.current_truth: 027 is the current refinement/release-alignment context; 026 is described as the completed predecessor.

findings.title: Obsolete 026 audit handoff
findings.stale_claim: Expected output is findings recorded in the 026 audit report, and troubleshooting says to rerun the 026 audit check.
findings.current_truth: 027 is the current refinement/release-alignment context; 026 is described as the completed predecessor.

findings.title: Obsolete 026 audit handoff
findings.stale_claim: Expected output is findings recorded in the 026 audit report, and troubleshooting says to rerun the 026 audit check.
findings.current_truth: 027 is the current refinement/release-alignment context; 026 is described as the completed predecessor.

findings.title: Obsolete 026 audit handoff
findings.stale_claim: Expected output is findings recorded in the 026 audit report, and troubleshooting says to rerun the 026 audit check.
findings.current_truth: 027 is the current refinement/release-alignment context; 026 is described as the completed predecessor.

findings.title: Obsolete 026 audit handoff
findings.stale_claim: Expected output is findings recorded in the 026 audit report, and troubleshooting says to rerun the 026 audit check.
findings.current_truth: 027 is the current refinement/release-alignment context; 026 is described as the completed predecessor.

findings.title: Obsolete 026 audit handoff
findings.stale_claim: Expected output is findings recorded in the 026 audit report, and troubleshooting says to rerun the 026 audit check.
findings.current_truth: 027 is the current refinement/release-alignment context; 026 is described as the completed predecessor.

findings.title: Create command roster omits parent-skill command
findings.stale_claim: The README says sk-doc is reached through six /create:* commands and lists /create:agent, /create:sk-skill, /create:feature-catalog, /create:testing-playbook, /create:folder_readme and /create:changelog.
findings.current_truth: The current create command group has seven shipped commands, including /create:sk-skill-parent.

findings.title: Spec-kit file contract is incomplete
findings.stale_claim: The README says system-spec-kit creates spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md and checklist.md.
findings.current_truth: Current Level 1 requires spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md and implementation-summary.md; Level 2 adds checklist.md; packet metadata also includes description.json and graph-metadata.json for phase parents and save/resume metadata.

findings.title: Code README still points at packet 026 audit output
findings.stale_claim: The usage example says expected output is recorded in the 026 audit report, and troubleshooting says to rerun the 026 audit check.
findings.current_truth: Current README rules say not to cite spec packet numbers, phase IDs or migration notes; use current behavior, stable paths and commands instead.

findings.title: Tests README still points at packet 026 audit output
findings.stale_claim: The usage example says findings are recorded in the 026 audit report, and troubleshooting says to rerun the 026 audit check.
findings.current_truth: Current README rules say not to cite spec packet numbers, phase IDs or migration notes; use current behavior, stable paths and commands instead.

findings.title: README guide hard-codes obsolete 9-section contract
findings.stale_claim: The guide says all READMEs follow a 9-section structure, with Quick Start, Features, Troubleshooting and related sections treated as broadly required.
findings.current_truth: The current README template says to use only sections that fit the audience, and code-folder READMEs must not force Quick Start, Features, FAQ or Troubleshooting unless earned.

findings.title: README guide has dead template section references
findings.stale_claim: The guide says readme_template.md Section 3 contains the section-by-type requirements matrix and Section 5 contains detailed must-include lists.
findings.current_truth: Current readme_template.md Section 3 is Skill and Project Profile, and Section 5 is Frontmatter; those referenced matrices/lists are no longer there.

findings.title: Spec-memory tool-count example is stale
findings.stale_claim: The README example table says MCP Tools = 33 across 7 layers.
findings.current_truth: The current spec-memory surface exposes 39 tools through the MCP registration and full-parity daemon-backed CLI.

findings.title: Valid README fixture endorses obsolete TOC anchors
findings.stale_claim: The fixture says a valid README has a Table of Contents and double-dash anchors.
findings.current_truth: Current README templates say not to add a Table of Contents or ANCHOR comments.

findings.title: $text mode contradicts current questioning rule
findings.stale_claim: The operating-modes table says $text is a direct prompt with no pre-processing question.
findings.current_truth: Current sk-prompt rules require one comprehensive question before processing, with $raw as the only documented exception.

findings.title: Small-model default framework claim misses Kimi
findings.stale_claim: The overview says MiniMax uses TIDD-EC, MiMo uses COSTAR and the rest default to RCAF.
findings.current_truth: Kimi-k2.7-code is active and uses COSTAR with TIDD-EC fallback while explicitly avoiding RCAF; only DeepSeek remains default-unverified RCAF.

findings.title: Verification section omits Kimi benchmark
findings.stale_claim: The verification section points readers only to benchmarks 003 and 004 for framework assignments.
findings.current_truth: The current model index and registry include an empirical Kimi-k2.7-code assignment backed by benchmark 007.

findings.title: GitHub auth troubleshooting points to non-existent config scopes
findings.stale_claim: For GitHub MCP 401/403 errors, regenerate the PAT and update `.utcp_config.json` with `repo`, `issues`, `pull_requests` and `workflow`.
findings.current_truth: The current Code Mode GitHub manual only wires `GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` through `.utcp_config.json`; those are PAT permissions/scopes to grant on the token, not fields to add to `.utcp_config.json`.

findings.title: Claude agent roster points at dead surface
findings.stale_claim: Claude Code agents live in `.claude/agents/*.md` and the README documents nine agents including `handover`, `research`, `speckit`, and `write`.
findings.current_truth: Current repo-local agent references point at `.opencode/agents/<name>.md`, and the actual roster includes current agents such as `markdown`, `deep-context`, `deep-improvement`, `prompt-improver`, and `code`; the README roster/path is stale.

findings.title: Codex profile model is stale
findings.stale_claim: Profiles live in `.codex/config.toml` under `[profiles.<name>]`, and the README documents six profiles.
findings.current_truth: The current repo config declares `[agents.*]` entries that point to `.codex/agents/*.toml`; the active Codex-side roster is larger and includes agents such as `code`, `deep-context`, `deep-improvement`, `deep-research`, `deep-review`, `markdown`, `orchestrate`, and `prompt-improver`.

findings.title: Agent flag guidance contradicts current routing
findings.stale_claim: The README says to omit `--agent` from every dispatch and later says never to pass a top-level `--agent`.
findings.current_truth: The current cli-opencode reference documents `--agent` as a supported core flag, uses a default dispatch shape with per-use-case `--agent <agent-slug>`, and routes concrete tasks through `--agent context|orchestrate|review|debug|ai-council`; only `general` and some small-model paths carry narrower omit-agent caveats.

findings.title: Provider matrix omits Kimi K2.7
findings.stale_claim: Seven providers are documented: `opencode-go`, `deepseek`, `minimax-coding-plan`, `minimax`, `xiaomi-token-plan-ams`, `xiaomi`, and `openai`.
findings.current_truth: The active small-model registry includes Kimi For Coding via provider `kimi-for-coding` with live slug `kimi-for-coding/k2p7`, and the cli-opencode model reference lists it as a dispatchable model path.

findings.title: ClickUp MCP quick start uses obsolete Code Mode invocation
findings.stale_claim: Register @clickup/mcp-server in platform MCP config with server key "clickup", then call Code Mode as call_tool_chain([{ tool: "clickup.clickup_create_document", input: ... }]).
findings.current_truth: Current Code Mode is wired through opencode.json to .utcp_config.json, the current ClickUp manual is named clickup_official, and call_tool_chain accepts an object with a code string, not an array of tool/input steps.

findings.title: OpenCode config example uses old mcpServers shape
findings.stale_claim: The OpenCode / Claude Code / Claude Desktop platform config example is a top-level mcpServers block.
findings.current_truth: Current OpenCode config uses a top-level mcp object with local server entries; Code Mode UTCP config uses manual_call_templates, not a top-level mcpServers block.

findings.title: Code Mode ClickUp examples use non-current manual name
findings.stale_claim: Examples discover and call clickup.clickup_create_task / clickup.clickup_* tools.
findings.current_truth: The current repo config names the ClickUp manual clickup_official, so clickup.clickup_* is not the current configured manual namespace.

findings.title: Native MCP roster omits Skill Advisor
findings.stale_claim: Native/direct MCP examples list Spec Kit Memory, Sequential Thinking and Code Graph, but omit Skill Advisor.
findings.current_truth: Current opencode.json also registers mk_skill_advisor as a native local MCP server.

findings.title: scripts README inventory is stale
findings.stale_claim: Key Statistics says Code files = 1 and Structure lists only validate_config.py; the README also routes follow-up to a 026 audit report/check.
findings.current_truth: The folder currently has install.sh, doctor.sh, update.sh and validate_config.py, and evergreen READMEs should not cite mutable packet/audit numbers.

findings.title: Chrome DevTools MCP version is wrong
findings.stale_claim: Register chrome-devtools-mcp@latest with --isolated=true in .utcp_config.json.
findings.current_truth: Current .utcp_config.json pins both chrome_devtools manuals to chrome-devtools-mcp@0.26.0 with --isolated=true.

findings.title: Figma MCP is not safely described as read-only
findings.stale_claim: The MCP is opt-in and read-only: pull design context into the agent for codegen.
findings.current_truth: Current docs confirm the live Figma manual includes download_figma_images for downloading referenced images/SVGs, and the install guide explicitly says not to assume a tool exists or is read-only.

findings.title: Open Design agent roster is narrower than current support
findings.stale_claim: MCP install needs the target agent CLI (opencode or claude).
findings.current_truth: Current wiring docs list supported agent slugs including claude, codex, cursor, copilot, openclaw, antigravity, gemini, pi, vibe, hermes, cline, kimi, trae and opencode.

findings.claim: The root README advertises 17 on-demand skills and a 37-tool memory surface.
findings.current_reality: README.md:874 and .opencode/skills/*/SKILL.md show 20 skills. spec-memory CLI parity tests assert exactly 39 tools at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/spec-memory-cli-parity-and-help.vitest.ts:122-128, and README.md:146/398 already describe 39 tools.
findings.impact: The top-level project overview gives stale primary-surface counts and contradicts later current-state text in the same file.
findings.recommendation: Update the banner and memory-layer table/surface prose to 20 skills and 39 spec-memory tools, or avoid exact counts in drift-prone overview copy.

findings.claim: AGENTS.md says spec-memory.cjs / mk-spec-memory expose 37 tools.
findings.current_reality: The current spec-memory tool surface is locked at 39 tools by .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/spec-memory-cli-parity-and-help.vitest.ts:122-128.
findings.impact: Agent routing/tooling instructions teach a stale MCP/CLI tool count for a mandatory surface.
findings.recommendation: Update the spec-memory count to 39 in both AGENTS.md locations.

findings.claim: The bin README says the spec-memory CLI has 37 tools and the offline smoke count is 37/8/9.
findings.current_reality: Current spec-memory parity is 39 tools; code-index and skill-advisor remain 8 and 9, making the current smoke tuple 39/8/9.
findings.impact: CLI launcher documentation and smoke-command comments are stale for the primary daemon CLI.
findings.recommendation: Update all repeated 37 and 37/8/9 references in this README to 39 and 39/8/9.

findings.claim: The install guide index says it covers 10 custom agents and that guide files are 5 real + 3 symlinks.
findings.current_reality: .opencode/agents/*.md contains 12 agents. The install_guides directory has 5 real markdown files and 5 markdown symlinks.
findings.impact: The install-guide index understates both the custom-agent surface and the guide inventory.
